skill.name: seedance-v2
skill.description: Generate cinematic short-form video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro on RunComfy. Documents Seedance 2.0 Pro's strengths (multi-modal references — up to 9 images, 3 videos, 3 audio — synchronized in-pass audio with natural lip-sync, cinematic motion refinement), the 4–15s duration schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Wan 2.7 / Kling instead. Calls `runcomfy run bytedance/seedance-v2/pro` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "seedance", "seedance 2", "seedance v2", "seedance pro", "bytedance video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic AI video with ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro including native lip-sync and multi-modal reference support.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is legitimate documentation for the RunComfy CLI video generation service. Static analysis flagged 89 potential issues, but evaluation reveals all are false positives: CLI command examples in documentation were misidentified as shell execution, hardcoded URLs are legitimate service endpoints, and filesystem references describe standard CLI configuration paths. No malicious intent detected. The skill safely documents API usage with proper security practices including HTTPS transmission and secure token storage.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Documentation Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 67 SKILL.md locations. These are documentation examples of CLI invocation syntax (e.g., `runcomfy run bytedance/seedance-v2/pro`). The skill is markdown documentation, not executable code. No actual shell execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation markdown describing CLI usage, not code that executes. Examples show bash command syntax for educational purposes.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Legitimate Service Endpoints
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Hardcoded URL' at 10 SKILL.md locations. These are documentation URLs for the RunComfy service (runcomfy.com, model-api.runcomfy.net) which is the legitimate third-party API this skill interfaces with.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs reference the legitimate RunComfy service and its documentation. No exfiltration to malicious domains detected.

low_findings.title: Path Placeholder Misidentified as Traversal
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Path traversal sequence' at SKILL.md:82, 95-97. The `<absolute/path>` is a documentation placeholder indicating where users should specify their output directory. No actual path traversal vulnerability exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation placeholder for user-specified output directory. Not a path traversal vulnerability since no user input is processed.

low_findings.title: CLI Configuration Path Documented
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Hidden file access' and 'Hidden file in home directory' at SKILL.md:177. This documents where the runcomfy CLI stores authentication tokens (`~/.config/runcomfy/token.json`). This is standard CLI configuration behavior, not a security vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation of CLI's secure token storage location with mode 0600 (owner-only). Standard practice for CLI tools.

content.user_title: Generate cinematic AI video with Seedance 2.0 Pro
content.value_statement: Creating professional short-form video content requires matching multiple reference assets to a coherent vision. Seedance 2.0 Pro enables this by combining up to 9 images, 3 videos, and 3 audio files into synchronized output with native lip-sync.
actual_capabilities: Generate 4-15 second cinematic video clips with text prompts up to 1000 words
Combine up to 9 reference images, 3 reference videos, and 3 audio files in a single generation
Produce native lip-synced audio with synchronized speech in the generated video
Support multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1, 21:9, and adaptive
Create consistent character identity using reference images across generations
Control output resolution at 480p or 720p with reproducibility via seed parameter
limitations: Maximum duration is 15 seconds; longer content requires multiple calls
Maximum resolution is 720p on the playground variant
Reference media must be 2-15 seconds for videos and under 15MB for audio
Lip-sync quality depends on prompt clarity and may not be perfect in all conditions

use_cases.title: Create spokesperson ads with consistent character identity
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can generate dialogue ads where a brand ambassador speaks with natural lip-sync. Upload a reference headshot to maintain consistent identity across multiple ad variations while adapting the message.

use_cases.title: Produce multilingual brand narratives
use_cases.description: Content creators can maintain brand consistency across languages by using reference images to hold identity while varying the prompt text for different language voiceovers and narration.

use_cases.title: Generate cinematic previs with camera direction
use_cases.description: Filmmakers can create pre-visualization shots by combining reference videos and audio to establish the look and feel of a scene before full production, with specific camera directions in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short video of [scene description]. Include [camera movement] and [lighting mood].

prompt_templates.title: Character-consistent spokesperson video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image_url for [reference headshot]. Generate a medium close-up where the person [action/dialogue]. Style: [tone description]. Lighting: [light setup].

prompt_templates.title: Multi-modal reference video
prompt_templates.prompt: Subject from image_url walks through [setting]. Match motion style from video_url. Voice tone matches audio_url. Cinematic camera: [shot description].

prompt_templates.title: Lip-synced dialogue ad
prompt_templates.prompt: Same person as image_url in [setting]. Medium close-up. Natural lip-sync. Say: [dialogue]. Tone: [delivery style]. Lighting: [description].

output_examples.input: Generate a barista explaining today's special with warm friendly tone, slow push-in, soft window light
output_examples.output: A 5-second video showing medium close-up of a barista in soft cafe lighting, naturally lip-synced explaining a menu item with gentle camera movement

output_examples.input: Product spokesperson ad with brand ambassador reference image and 9:16 vertical format
output_examples.output: A vertical video maintaining consistent character identity with the reference image, delivering promotional dialogue with professional lip-sync and soft lighting

best_practices: Use image references for stable identity elements like faces, costumes, and brand marks rather than describing them in text
Write camera and motion directions in plain language like 'slow push-in', 'locked tripod', or 'handheld follow'
Match aspect ratio of reference media to desired output to avoid unwanted cropping
anti_patterns: Avoid mixing reference styles that conflict drastically (like watercolor and photorealistic) as this confuses the model
Do not try to verbally describe a character's face or identity; use image_url instead for consistent results
Do not request videos longer than 15 seconds; segment into multiple calls for longer content

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration?
faq.answer: The maximum duration is 15 seconds. For longer content, you must generate multiple clips and splice them together.

faq.question: How does lip-sync work?
faq.answer: When generate_audio is true (default), Seedance 2.0 Pro creates synchronized speech during generation. The prompt describes the tone and content, and the model produces matching lip movement.

faq.question: Can I maintain consistent character identity across videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Upload a reference image to image_url for characters, costumes, or brand elements. The model uses this to maintain identity across generations.

faq.question: What formats are supported for reference images?
faq.answer: Reference images can be JPEG, PNG, WebP, BMP, TIFF, or GIF. Up to 9 images can be used simultaneously.

faq.question: How do I set up the RunComfy CLI?
faq.answer: Install with 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli', then run 'runcomfy login' for interactive authentication. In CI environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What happens if my reference media exceeds size limits?
faq.answer: Reference videos must be 2-15 seconds and reference audio must be under 15MB and 2-15 seconds. Out-of-range files will be rejected with an error.
